channel.title: Dragonlance
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Alarl
item.description:
Alarl is one of the smaller isles of the eight isles located in the Dragon Isles. It is located south of Mind, and south and east of Misty Isle. This island is a dumping point for ships coming to the Dragon Isles. If they have any Kender on their ships, they stop here and drop them off first at the town of Perch.
The isle is covered with gentle hills, and the entire island is cover in woodlands that aren't dense. The lowlands that are on the island tend to be marshlands with all the rivers and lakes that cover the island. The only places that do not have trees on them are almost all the hilltops, where Metallic Dragons can find easy places to land.
No clan of dragons claims this island, but treat it as more of a park. Here dragons will come and spend years at a time to see the wildlife here and the inhabitants of the island making a living for themselves. Many different types of creatures inhabit the island such as antelope, deer, goats, bears, wolves, all types of birds, many types of feline predators, and elephants. Also there are small tribes of Kagonesti, Humans, and Kyrie (Krynn's word for Aarakocra) that are rarely seen in the forests.
Ararl is an isle of ruins. Nothing tragic happened, it just lies uninhabited with no wildlife nor any other life forms. Nature has reclaimed almost all the land with small remnants of old homes, shops, and other buildings remaining.

item.title: Berann
item.description:
Berann is one of the smaller isles of the eight isles located in the Dragon Isles. It is located between the isles of Tayol and Jaentarth, and south of Winged Majesty. This isle is also where the Order of Brass is based off of along with being the home of brass dragons.
The island is cut in half from west to east by a series of high, rocky ridges that are high and very sharp. Here is where most of the brass dragons can be found to have homes in. All over the island are hundreds of small villages that are inhabited by humans. Brass dragons are known to adopt one or two of these villages, bringing fresh kills to the inhabitants in exchanged for some baked goods.
Berann is where most of the young Manaketes spend their days, playing and running like kids do. Aside from their homogeneity, it's kids being kids.

item.title: Dragon Isles
item.description:
The Dragon Isles lie eighty miles to the north of the Cape of Nordmaar. Little is known about these islands—they are all but impossible to reach as they are magically protected. Near the Isles a giant leviathan lives that is called the Golden Whale.
The islands are located in the center of the topics—the center of Krynn. The good dragons inhabit the City of Gold, capital of Misty Isle, though little is known of them. However, after the destruction of the Irda prior to the Chaos War, little is known about the fate of the islands.
While the Islands have since been repurposed to be a haven for the Manaketes, they keep historical records of the islands before and slightly after the cataclysm before they sealed themselves behind the mist and the protection of the Golden Whale.

item.title: Heart
item.description:
Heart is one of the smaller isles of the eight isles located in the Dragon Isles. The isle is located between the large isles of Winged Majesty to the west and Misty Isle to the east. Many different beings live on the isle including Dwarves, Elves, Humans, Kender, centaurs, Minotaurs, Goblins, Hobgoblins, Ogres, and half-ogres, all who are seeking a life of solitude. It is not uncommon to find very small villages of multiple races living together in peace.
The isle doesn't have high mountains as most of the other isles do. Instead, it has some steep hills with sharp cliffs that fall off into gorges. The forest there is very dense and completely covers the entire island. Passage through the forests are very hard, but an abundance of food can be found in the forest at any given time of the year. There is no place on the island where a ship can safely sail up to the island, but rather they have to anchor offshore and take a small boat to the beach.
What lies on Heart now is known only to the council and those they allow to visit.

item.title: Jaentharth
item.description:
Jaentarth is one of the smaller isles of the eight isles located in the Dragon Isles. It is located south of Misty Isle and Heart, and east of Berann. The isle is home to Humans and Minotaurs whom are the descendants of pirates and shipwrecked mariners. For the metallic dragons, Copper Dragons, Silver Dragons, and Brass Dragons call this place home.
Jaentarth is a rugged land, with many cloud-caped peaks. Below the peaks, the isle is covered in dense jungle. The shores around the isle are very rocky, and have been known to destroy many ships that have ventured too close. The shores do not have an area on the island that is suitable for a harbor for any ship, and no cities are known to be located along the coastline. In the mountains it is very cold, while below them the jungle is very hot and humid.
Jaentharth is now an island home to 5 stone statues of serpentine-like dragons, believed to be statues of the Daughters of Paladine.

item.title: Mind
item.description:
Mind is the third largest isle of the eight isles located in the Dragon Isles. The isle is located northeast of Misty Isle, and north of Alarl. The isle is run by Copper Dragons, and there are more on this isle than on any place on Krynn. The copper clans live in a rigid clan hierarchy, with the older dragons
The isle of Mind is almost completely cut in two by two deep fjords that cut into the isle from the east and west. Inside these fjords, there are no beaches, but high, sheer cliffs. The landscape of the isle has thick jungle along the coastline that gives away to tall mountains in the interior. The mountains though, are not high enough to be snow capped. The copper dragons make their homes in the caves of the mountains.
There are other people living on the isle other than just dragons. The most prominent are Humans, mostly Warriors from the Legion of Steel. They are located in the western half of the isle, living in small villages. Each village teaches a different ability to a warrior. Located deeper in the island are small bands of Kyrie and shadowpeople (ancient, reputedly harmless Ogres).
Now, Mind is a place where Manaketes go to meditate for up to 12 hours a day. It is a symbol of reconnecting with nature and the world as a whole, seen as a form of enlightenment.

item.title: Misty Isle
item.description:
Misty Isle is covered with every type of land feature that all the other isles have. They have two very high, snow-capped mountain ranges that travel from east to west and meet at each coastline. The coastlines range from white sandy beaches, to high cliffs where landfall would be impossible. The island has several deep fjords that go into the isle, and there are many freshwater streams and rivers that flow into the ocean, but not many freshwater lakes. There are deep, verdant forests along the coastlines, but a person is able to walk through them with little trouble. Many roads crisscross throughout the isle, with many villages located at the crossroads of these roads. Food is plentiful, with many great herds of deer and buffalo, and plenty of fresh fruits can be harvested.
Today, Misty Isle is the home of the ancient capital of Aurialastican, where all the Manaketes live their lives peacefully.

item.title: Tayol
item.description:
Tayol is one of the larger isles of the Dragon Isles. Tayol is the western most of the isles, west of Winged Majesty and Berann. No other races of beings live on the isle other than Bronze Dragons except in the only city on the isle, Lief.
Much of the isle is nothing more than lowlands and swamps. There are several fjords that form great marsh-lined salt flats, and are subjected to many great tidal surges. There are some mountains in the interior that form bowl shaped valleys that are filled with swamplands.
Reently, Tayol has transformed into an island filled with volcanic rocks that is inhabited with mostly large wildlife such as boars and large cats such as tigers and panthers.

item.title: Winged Majesty
item.description:
Winged Majesty is the second largest of all the isles of the Dragon Isles. It is located east of Tayol, north of Berann, and west of Heart. This isle is home to many Silver Dragons who live in the lofty mountains, and Kagonesti Elves who live in the jungles.
In modern times, Winged Majesty is home to open plains where much wildlife fly and roam freely.

item.title: Auri
item.description: Standing at the center of the council, Auri appears around seventeen with white-gold hair and molten amber eyes marked by narrow vertical pupils. She speaks softly and listens far more than she talks, usually watching others with quiet curiosity. While she outwardly presents herself as simply another Manakete, she naturally becomes the center of attention during discussions, and the others often look to her before decisions are finalized. Calm, thoughtful, and emotionally perceptive, Auri tends to weigh long-term consequences and philosophical questions before acting.

item.title: Karef
item.description: Karef appears about fifteen with golden-tan skin, short white-silver hair, and warm sunlit yellow eyes ringed faintly in amber. He usually carries a gentle half-smile and a relaxed posture, often seated cross-legged when at ease. Karef is the council’s mediator and conversationalist, preferring to understand people through dialogue rather than confrontation. Patient and perceptive, he asks thoughtful questions that reveal motivations beneath the surface. When disagreements arise among the council, he often tempers Burll’s caution and Turq’s severity, keeping discussions grounded and constructive.

item.title: Maris
item.description: The broadest of the group, Maris appears around sixteen with silver hair pulled loosely back and storm-grey eyes touched with faint teal. His stance is steady and grounded, giving the impression of someone used to standing his ground against powerful forces. Practical and protective, Maris approaches most situations cautiously and often voices the first concerns when outsiders arrive. He remembers the consequences of misplaced trust and tends to assume the worst until proven otherwise, acting as the council’s defensive voice.

item.title: Serelyn
item.description: The smallest of the council and appearing around twelve, Serelyn has silver-white hair worn simply and pale moonlit-blue eyes with sharp vertical slit pupils. She carries herself with straight posture and quiet composure, often standing with her hands folded while listening. Among the group she is the most morally principled, closely aligned with Paladine’s ideals of protection and restraint. She watches how the party treats others and quickly grows cold toward arrogance or cruelty. Despite her youthful appearance she speaks with calm clarity and often challenges the others when discussions turn overly cynical.

item.title: Veyrin
item.description: Veyrin appears seventeen, lean and relaxed with platinum hair that often falls loosely across his brow and verdigris-green eyes flecked with copper. A crooked smile and casual posture make him seem irreverent, but he is one of the council’s most observant members. He enjoys probing conversations, subtly testing people’s consistency and motivations with clever questions or dry humor. Veyrin often spots tensions or contradictions others miss, making him particularly attentive to divisions within the party.

item.title: 64. February 28th, 2026 The Scales of Silence
item.description:
Arriving on the shore, Krynn's Vanguard was greeted by three elf-like creatures who looked very similar. The three of them looked no older than seventeen. One looked extremely young, no older than twelve. Each one was of a porcelain complexion with platinum, silver, or white hair. They stood no taller than 5'6" and were very lean in build. Their ears were not as elongated as elf ears, but were pointed. However, instead of angled back, their ears pointed straight up, maybe only about an inch or so from where a human ear would end. The only distinct difference among them were their eyes. Their irises seemed to stir and mix on their own with a vertical pupil pointed at the ends much like a reptile's eye.
After being asked for their name and their purpose, the tone changed slightly. More deliberate, a bit wary. Those who greeted them gave them small, beaded threads they called 'bindings'. They were symbolic symbols that they would not draw or use their weapons for violence. They asked the party to wrap them around the hilts of their weapons as a show of good faith, and they complied.
Being led up to the city, Krynn's Vanguard noticed that the city was not built into the enviornment, but around it. And looking at the population, it was young. No elderly people, no one appeared to be over 30 and they all looked the same. Not the same, but very homogeneous. It was a bit of a shock. Two of the three left to go talk to the council and left them with the girl who appeared to be no older than twelve. She introduced herself as Serelyn and told them they should get them a change of clothes. Everyone wore the same soft, pale fabrics — platinum, ash, muted silver — draped in clean lines that fall naturally rather than sharply tailored. The outer layer wraps across the chest and fastens without visible ornament. Sleeves hang loose at the wrist, long enough to soften movement but never cumbersome. The garments are neither elaborate nor plain. They are intentional. No embroidery for status. No color denoting rank. No sigils of office. The silhouette is the same from child to elder. Differences are subtle — a shift in fabric weight, a slightly varied fold, faint metallic thread along a seam. But the structure remains constant. It is not a uniform enforced by law. It is a form chosen by everyone.
A tailor a bit into town was able to give them the same clothing calling it "Auravethra". When the party changed their clothes, they noticed their armor, equipment, and everything else melded into the Auravethra, almost as if it had disappeared and the Auravethra was all they had on. After a while, the other two who greeted them told them the council would speak with them in three days time. In the meantime, they were welcome to explore all the islands as they pleased, as long as they remained off the last island, known as Heart. Given a map, Orla and Armantaro explored the islands as Kyan Covverre and Orion went rock climbing along the cliffs, and Big Bob kept cooking with what he could find.
After a few days, and exploring the islands, the most interesting thing was 5 statues of serpentine-like dragons on Jaentharth, the seventh island. It was then time for them to meet the coucil. A messenger was sent to get them and bring them up a spiral carved into the largest tree on the island that stands above the rest of the canopy. Carved into it, was a chamber that opens to a view of the ocean. There, 5 figures stood. One they had met before.
From left to right they introduced themselves. Quick and simple. Serelyn stood on the left, next to her was Karef, the girl in the middle did not speak. To her left, the party's right, stood Veyrin, and finally Maris. The girl in the middle, presumably the leader introduced herslef as Auri. She asked the party why they came. She already knew, just wanted to make sure their aim was as they stated before.
After talking, the group seemed to argue among themselves, Maris being the one most strongly against letting them even get close to the shard, Auri stood silent. Maris, being frustrated finally turned to Auri and asked "You don't think this is coincidence?". Having never took her eyes off the party she responded simply 'No.'
Making the decision, Auri would take them to the shard the next day. That day came and sure enough, Auri took them to the island of Heart. Heart was very much left as a natural habitat, but there was no wildlife, under the brush was a stone cavern. Auri stopped and turned to them "Inside you'll find the shard. This is as far as I'm allowed to go." With that, the party decended. Reaching an opening, to the northwest, northeast, southwest, and southeast were 4 hallways. Directly ahead, were two large, wooden doors. The doors were locked. Krynn's Vanguard took the northwest path.
After a while, they came to a room with a Red Dragon chained and bound, heavily wounded. It didn't attack. It looked defeated, and as if it was begging for death. The party knew that this dragon, although they'd never met it, had committed many atrocities. After a long debate, they loosened the dragons restraints, but did not harm it nor release it.
The dragon stood, unfurled it's wings and flapped them three times before it disappeared in a flash of light. A flat stone in the back of the room, about 30 feet up had an inscription carved in it 'Violence mastered'. The path the party took them reopened. What would the next trial hold?
item.guid: Session Notes/64. February 28th, 2026 The Scales of Silence.md
